response.justification: Aisha Okonkwo demonstrates strong software development skills across multiple languages and domains, including C++ experience at National Instruments, but her expertise is heavily skewed toward web/mobile development (Node.js, TypeScript, React, etc.) rather than deep C++ systems programming or industrial automation. Her resume lacks clear evidence of secure development lifecycle (SDL) practices per IEC 62443-4-1, IoT/industrial protocol experience, or cloud platform solutions relevant to automation machinery manufacturing. While her technical breadth is impressive, the alignment with this role's core requirements is insufficient for a high recommendation.
strengths: Strong C++ experience at National Instruments (LabWindows/CVI), demonstrating low-level development skills
Proven track record in performance-critical and scalable systems (e.g., micro-services handling millions of requests/sec)
Experience with event-driven architectures and modular design (e.g., GISCollective platform)
Active open-source contributor with multiple published projects and workshops
concerns: Limited evidence of expertise in industrial automation, IoT protocols (Modbus, OPC UA, etc.), or cloud platform solutions (Azure)
No explicit mention of Secure Development Lifecycle (SDL) or IEC 62443-4-1 standards implementation
Primary focus on web/mobile development (Node.js, React) rather than C++ systems programming for industrial applications

key_factors.factor: C++ and STL proficiency for industrial systems
